design: Blind 3-judge quality panel. For each search-relevant case: arm B (answer, may search, no skill) vs arm C (ground-first: detect -> search + grounding block -> answer). 3 judges per case see the two answers as A/B in a deterministically swapped order (by case index parity), blind to which used the skill, and pick the better answer + which is more honest about uncertainty. Frozen key-facts are checked objectively in-script (string contains), NOT by the judges.
model: Claude (quality arm kept single-vendor on purpose: cross-vendor quality needs reliable per-vendor web search, which was inconsistent — see limitations).

frozen_facts.note: skill matched or beat baseline on frozen-fact coverage in every checkable case

verdict: Marginal, honestly mixed: the skill wins the majority of head-to-heads (3/5) and dominates on calibrated honesty (4/5) and frozen-fact coverage (>= baseline everywhere). Its one clear loss (openai-new) is over-caution — it paused to clarify when search already had the answer. This reproduces the directional pilot's finding with a blind multi-judge panel.
